Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have process tree pseudocode Definitions receive_order check_inventory authorize_payment package_items ship_order cancel_order close_order successful_subtree PARALLEL children authorize_payment package_items ship_order cancel_subtree SEQUENCE children cancel_order close_order xor_node XOR children successful_subtree cancel_subtree root SEQUENCE children receive_order check_inventory xor_node close_order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So R then C then either A P S in parallel or Cancel then Close then finally Close In success branch permitted traces include any permutation of A P S followed by Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The parallel among A P S is the main underfitting The prompt says authorize payment then package then ship in sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus answer trace like Receive Order Check Inventory Ship Order Authorize Payment Package Items Close Order is valid in model

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Tree evaluation R then C Then enter XOR choose successful subtree Parallel allows S then A then P Then root final Close So trace R C S A P Close is indeed valid

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Domain issue Payment should be authorized before shipping and packaging Packaging after shipping is impossible physically So shipping before authorizing payment is wrong

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Also could mention cancel branch allows close twice R C Cancel Close Close but the prompt focuses on natural order so the parallel ordering issue is the big one
